title: Add SLO→Customer Impact Classifier & Remediation Workflow doc (v2 scoring + updated comms)
preview:
Context
We have an internal Confluence page describing the SLO→customer impact tier classifier and the remediation workflow. That page is being referenced during incidents, but the operational details have drifted since the enrichment pipeline shipped score weighting changes (v2) and CS escalation timing was tighten...
text:
Context
We have an internal Confluence page describing the SLO→customer impact tier classifier and the remediation workflow. That page is being referenced during incidents, but the operational details have drifted since the enrichment pipeline shipped score weighting changes (v2) and CS escalation timing was tightened after SUP-19344.

This PR adds the canonical doc to the slo-toolkit repo so it can be versioned alongside the classifier code and reviewed via normal PR workflows. The majority of the content mirrors the Confluence page, but includes the v2 scoring thresholds and updated templates.

What changed (summary)
- Keep: tier definitions (1–4), overall workflow (alert→enrich→score→tier→remediate), evidence retention requirements, and post-incident/error budget reconciliation flow.
- Update: score mapping thresholds (v2), add confidence gating and a human-review fallback, update ...

title: Compact call-tree capture and on-call primer seeder
preview: Motivation: On-call responders frequently need a quick top-down view of what the service process looked like at incident start (goroutine/thread hotspots, recent exec path, open network sockets) and a focused, action-oriented primer tailored to the observed signals. This PR introduces a lightweight call-tree collect...
text:
Motivation: On-call responders frequently need a quick top-down view of what the service process looked like at incident start (goroutine/thread hotspots, recent exec path, open network sockets) and a focused, action-oriented primer tailored to the observed signals. This PR introduces a lightweight call-tree collector and an on-call primer seeder that bundles captured artifacts with heuristic remediation hints so first responders can act faster without full trace access.

What this does: 1) Introduces a compact call-tree capture agent (golang) that samples active goroutines+stack traces, condenses them into a weighted call-tree (hot path aggregation), and attaches minor environment fingerprints (listening ports, recent open files, binary version). 2) Adds a seeder component (python) that consumes the compact capture and generates a short on-call primer: probable root causes, 3 next di...

title: heuristic DAG runbook compiler and local repro hints
preview: Motivation: Oncall responders frequently need a concise, executable set of steps that adapt to observed telemetry. This PR introduces a heuristic DAG compiler that converts high-level runbook templates and telemetry predicates into compact conditional playbooks plus short local repro hints (minimal traces, query sni...
text: Motivation: Oncall responders frequently need a concise, executable set of steps that adapt to observed telemetry. This PR introduces a heuristic DAG compiler that converts high-level runbook templates and telemetry predicates into compact conditional playbooks plus short local repro hints (minimal traces, query snippets, and service call stubs). Design: 1) A lightweight DAG IR captures nodes (check, query, mitigation, escalate) and edges with guard predicates; 2) Compiler heuristics prune nodes unlikely to apply given recent telemetry (configurable thresholds) and collapse linear chains into single composite actions to reduce cognitive load; 3) Local repro hint extractor synthesizes a minimal set of queries/trace spans and a docker-compose sandbox recipe (when available) to reproduce the symptom locally; 4) Safe-mode runner executes a dry-run passing through the DAG with simulated te...

title: compact-shift-briefs: lightweight handoff digest and stratified probe sampler
preview:
Summary: This PR introduces `shift-brief`, a lightweight CLI and library for creating compact on-call handoff digests, plus a companion `stratified-probe-sampler` that schedules small, targeted telemetry probes during shift boundaries.

Motivation: long handoff notes and oversized telemetry bundles increase cognitiv...
text:
Summary: This PR introduces `shift-brief`, a lightweight CLI and library for creating compact on-call handoff digests, plus a companion `stratified-probe-sampler` that schedules small, targeted telemetry probes during shift boundaries.

Motivation: long handoff notes and oversized telemetry bundles increase cognitive load for responders and slow down triage during rotations. The goal here is to reduce the reading time and bandwidth for on-call handoffs while retaining actionable context and safe, opt-in telemetry links.

Implementation highlights:
- New CLI command `shift-brief generate` (flags: --window, --severity-threshold, --sanitize-level) that emits a 3–5 sentence summary, prioritized list of active incidents (id, severity, 1-line status), and 1–2 sanitized example traces. Summaries are generated by heuristics tuned for recent incident activity and enriched with SLO drift metada...

title: Introduce alert-load-simulator and alert-bias-metrics for on-call calibration
preview:
Background and motivation:
We frequently see skew between alert load observed during traffic spikes and the expected on-call burden derived from static alert rates. That mismatch causes under-provisioned paging windows and creates noisy escalation paths. This PR adds a lightweight alert-load-simulator and a small su...
text:
Background and motivation:
We frequently see skew between alert load observed during traffic spikes and the expected on-call burden derived from static alert rates. That mismatch causes under-provisioned paging windows and creates noisy escalation paths. This PR adds a lightweight alert-load-simulator and a small suite of alert-bias-metrics to help SREs and product teams reason about how configuration changes, SLO adjustments, and alert tuning affect on-call load under realistic failure and traffic patterns.

What this PR does (high level):
- Adds a simulator harness (slo_toolkit.simulator) that can replay historical incidents or generate synthetic traffic/failure scenarios to estimate page counts, paging latency distributions, and concurrency of alerts.
- Ships a set of alert-bias metrics (slo_toolkit.bias) which estimate the difference between observed page volume and expected volum...

title: Stratified alert degrader and structured handoff manifest
preview: Context: Pager fatigue remains a major operational cost for services with noisy, low-impact alerts. This PR implements a stratified alert degrader that automatically reduces noisy signals to digestible summaries or lower-churn channels when they don't change SLO posture and responders have an elevated load. It also ...
text: Context: Pager fatigue remains a major operational cost for services with noisy, low-impact alerts. This PR implements a stratified alert degrader that automatically reduces noisy signals to digestible summaries or lower-churn channels when they don't change SLO posture and responders have an elevated load. It also introduces a structured 'handoff manifest' artifact that the on-call rotation system and runbook generator can consume to present a compact, actionable snapshot for the incoming shift. What this PR does: 1) New degrader engine (slo-toolkit/alert_degrader) with pluggable policies based on SLO impact, recent incident frequency, and on-call availability. 2) Exporter that synthesizes a JSON handoff manifest (slo-toolkit/handoff/manifest_v1) containing prioritized alerts, suggested runbook entry, last-triage snapshot, and responsible owners. 3) CLI entrypoint (slo-manifestctl) a...

title: Playbook canary runner and sandboxed rehearsal CLI
preview: Motivation: Runbooks are frequently edited without a lightweight way to validate that the sequence of steps and automated actions behave as expected. This PR introduces a canary runner for scheduled playbook rehearsals and a sandboxed CLI (slo-rehearse) that lets oncall and SREs dry-run playbooks against a mocked en...
text:
Motivation: Runbooks are frequently edited without a lightweight way to validate that the sequence of steps and automated actions behave as expected. This PR introduces a canary runner for scheduled playbook rehearsals and a sandboxed CLI (slo-rehearse) that lets oncall and SREs dry-run playbooks against a mocked environment. Goals: 1) validate runbook logic and step ordering, 2) surface flaky remediation actions before hitting production, 3) provide safe rehearsal that exercises telemetry and alerting hooks without creating real incidents.

What this PR does: adds a sandboxed rehearsal CLI (slo-rehearse) that boots an ephemeral sandbox with mock providers, a playbook-canary scheduler/runner that can be configured per-service to run a subset of playbooks as periodic canaries, and a set of safety gates and reversible mocks so rehearsals cannot modify production state. The CLI supports ...

title: add-synthetic-alert-replayer-and-slo-drift-detector-with-cli-integration
preview: Motivation: On-call engineers frequently need to understand whether recent config/traffic changes produced real SLO regressions or flurries of noisy alerts. This PR introduces two complementary utilities in slo-toolkit: (1) a synthetic alert replayer that can replay historical alert batches against current routing/a...
text:
Motivation: On-call engineers frequently need to understand whether recent config/traffic changes produced real SLO regressions or flurries of noisy alerts. This PR introduces two complementary utilities in slo-toolkit: (1) a synthetic alert replayer that can replay historical alert batches against current routing/alerting logic (useful to validate alert rules and noise filters), and (2) an SLO drift detector that computes lightweight drift signals across sliding windows and surfaces candidate regressions with example traces and suggested runbook links. Both are exposed via a small CLI shim (sloctl replay / sloctl drift) and wired into the existing incident-bot runbook hinting path so that alerts replayed in a dry-run mode can annotate runbook suggestions with a confidence score.

What this PR does (single continuous description): Adds a new package slo_toolkit/replay that ingests ale...

title: Add audit log delivery SLO (latency + error rate) with enterprise-tier burn alerts
preview:
Summary
This PR adds an official SLO definition for the audit log delivery pipeline in slo-toolkit. The SLO covers (1) delivery latency and (2) delivery error rate (drops/failures), and includes error-budget burn alerts tuned for enterprise-tier expectations.

Context / why
As we standardize audit logging for Hosted...
text:
Summary
This PR adds an official SLO definition for the audit log delivery pipeline in slo-toolkit. The SLO covers (1) delivery latency and (2) delivery error rate (drops/failures), and includes error-budget burn alerts tuned for enterprise-tier expectations.

Context / why
As we standardize audit logging for Hosted/Dedicated/Private, audit delivery becomes a compliance-relevant pipeline. We need a single SLO that SRE + Security Eng can reference, and that we can consistently deploy across regions/environments. This SLO is meant to pair with the new audit pipeline dashboards/alerts work (observability-pack) and reduce alert noise by tightening conditions for enterprise while keeping standard tiers actionable.

What’s included
- New SLO: audit_log_delivery
 - Latency SLI: fraction of audit events delivered to the ingestion backend within threshold (derived from audit delivery latency h...

title: Metering data quality SLO template (freshness + correctness) with example alerts
preview:
Context
We’ve had recurring issues where metering tables are “up” but not trustworthy: late-arriving events, duplicates due to retry/backfill paths, and dimensional nulls (e.g., region) causing downstream drift vs billing ledger. Finance/RevRec needs a consistent, auditable definition for: (1) how fresh billing data...
text:
Context
We’ve had recurring issues where metering tables are “up” but not trustworthy: late-arriving events, duplicates due to retry/backfill paths, and dimensional nulls (e.g., region) causing downstream drift vs billing ledger. Finance/RevRec needs a consistent, auditable definition for: (1) how fresh billing datasets must be to be used for invoice preview / month-end close and (2) what correctness signals we monitor to catch billing-impacting issues early.

This PR adds a reusable SLO template focused on metering data quality, intended to be imported by services/pipelines that publish billing-critical tables (usage events, reconciliation exports, and ledger join views). The template standardizes:
- Freshness SLIs/SLOs (time since last complete partition; late event window)
- Correctness signals (drift vs ledger, duplicate event rate, null/missing dimension rate)
- Example alert rul...

title: ephemeral-diagnostic-funnel-and-priority-playbook-serializer
Triage Wizard: ephemeral telemetry aggregator + guided playbook preview
preview: This PR introduces a 'Triage Wizard' micro-workflow for on-call responders that assembles a compact, ephemeral telemetry snapshot and renders a guided playbook preview pre-populated with suggested remedial steps. Motivation: reduce cognitive overhead during incident triage by surfacing only the most relevant metrics...
text: This PR introduces a 'Triage Wizard' micro-workflow for on-call responders that assembles a compact, ephemeral telemetry snapshot and renders a guided playbook preview pre-populated with suggested remedial steps. Motivation: reduce cognitive overhead during incident triage by surfacing only the most relevant metrics, traces, and recent errors for the alert context, and to let responders preview playbook steps with example commands before executing. The core pieces: 1) telemetry-aggregator service: background job that collects top-5 signals (latency p50/p95, error trends, recent traces, host counts, queue depth) and outputs a compressed JSON blob; 2) triage-preview UI: client component that renders the snapshot alongside a stepwise playbook with inline 'try this' commands; 3) CLI hook: ability to fetch ephemeral snapshot and run a local dry-run of suggested remediation; 4) safety and p...

title: telemetry-guided pretriage sanity-checker and lightweight oncall hook
preview: Motivation: on-call teams spend time investigating noisy alerts that are detectable as instrumentation anomalies (missing series, stale exporters, or thin traffic windows) before starting a full incident triage. This PR adds a lightweight 'pretriage sanity checker' that runs a small set of telemetry probes when an a...
text: Motivation: on-call teams spend time investigating noisy alerts that are detectable as instrumentation anomalies (missing series, stale exporters, or thin traffic windows) before starting a full incident triage. This PR adds a lightweight 'pretriage sanity checker' that runs a small set of telemetry probes when an alert is received and surfaces a short human-readable verdict and artifacts to the responder. Design summary: implement a probe runner (timeout-first, non-blocking) that evaluates a configurable probe set against Prometheus and tracing backends, computes a compact fingerprint, and emits a JSON snapshot + human summary. Ship a CLI hook and a webhook payload for incident-bot/slo-toolkit to call as part of pretriage. Safety and opt-in: default checks are safe read-only Prometheus queries and a trace existence probe; probes and timeouts are configurable per-team via runtime conf...

title: Add rollout-scoped burn-rate endpoint (route/model_version/region) with query caching
preview: Summary: This adds first-class support in slo-toolkit for querying burn-rate scoped to a rollout slice (route + model_version + region + optional customer_tier). The rollout controller needs to evaluate fast/slow burn windows against the exact scope of the rollout step; previously we only supported coarse service-le...
text:
Summary: This adds first-class support in slo-toolkit for querying burn-rate scoped to a rollout slice (route + model_version + region + optional customer_tier). The rollout controller needs to evaluate fast/slow burn windows against the exact scope of the rollout step; previously we only supported coarse service-level burn-rate and a handful of static labels, which caused both false positives (baseline traffic in other regions) and false negatives (rollout-only regression diluted by global volume). This PR introduces (1) a new endpoint that accepts rollout scope and returns multi-window burn rate + sample sizes, (2) a query-planning layer to avoid N+1 PromQL fanout, and (3) an in-process cache + singleflight to reduce backend load and smooth bursty controller polling.

API changes: - New endpoint: GET /v1/burn-rate/rollout?service=<svc>&route=<route>&slo=<slo_name>&model_version=<ver...

title: Add incident remediation SLA policy evaluator (due times + breach classification)
preview: Context: As part of the Reliability Program (Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping), we need a single, audited implementation for remediation SLA timers. Today, multiple systems (incident-bot, dashboards, ad-hoc scripts) are calculating “SLA due” inconsistently, and edge cases (pause/override, force majeure, custome...
text: Context: As part of the Reliability Program (Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping), we need a single, audited implementation for remediation SLA timers. Today, multiple systems (incident-bot, dashboards, ad-hoc scripts) are calculating “SLA due” inconsistently, and edge cases (pause/override, force majeure, customer-caused) are handled differently. This PR introduces a small, dependency-light library in slo-toolkit to: (1) compute due_at for remediation SLAs from incident metadata, (2) evaluate whether an incident is currently in breach / at risk, and (3) provide a stable, versioned policy surface we can share across services. Scope / Approach: - Adds @redwood/sla-policy (library) with a compact policy model that takes severity (Sev0–Sev3), tier (Hosted | Dedicated | Private), and taxonomy (incident_type + optional subtype) and returns: time_to_mitigate_minutes, time_to_fix_minutes, ...

title: CLI: generate tiered SLO configs + validate metric coverage by tier/endpoint/region
preview: This PR adds CLI tooling to (1) generate tier-aware SLO config files from a canonical targets table and (2) validate metric coverage required to compute SLIs per tier/endpoint/region. This is intended to unblock the Tiered SLO + Error Budget program by making SLO config generation repeatable and preventing silent SL...
text: This PR adds CLI tooling to (1) generate tier-aware SLO config files from a canonical targets table and (2) validate metric coverage required to compute SLIs per tier/endpoint/region. This is intended to unblock the Tiered SLO + Error Budget program by making SLO config generation repeatable and preventing silent SLO data gaps (e.g., missing tier tags at ingest, missing streaming disconnect counters, or latency histograms not exported in a region). The generator produces configs compatible with the redwood SLO loader/schema introduced in redwood/pr-28438 (same field names and tier labeling). The validator checks that required metrics exist and are queryable for each config row, with guardrails to avoid cardinality explosions (no per-customer/per-model checks by default; validation is done at metric+label-key level). The CLI is intended for CI usage (fail fast) and for on-call debuggin...

title: Add error budget burn-rate alerts for enterprise protected-route SLOs
preview:
## Context
This PR adds burn-rate alerting presets to `slo-toolkit` for Redwood Hosted API enterprise protected routes as part of the Hot-route capacity protection initiative. The intent is to page on *fast* error-budget consumption for enterprise SLOs (e.g., /v1/chat/completions, embeddings, rerank) without generat...
text:
## Context
This PR adds burn-rate alerting presets to `slo-toolkit` for Redwood Hosted API enterprise protected routes as part of the Hot-route capacity protection initiative. The intent is to page on *fast* error-budget consumption for enterprise SLOs (e.g., /v1/chat/completions, embeddings, rerank) without generating paging noise during expected best-effort shedding.

Motivation:
- Prior incident and game-day findings showed we need early, reliable paging on *enterprise* SLO risk rather than generic fleet-wide latency/5xx signals.
- With tier-aware throttling/admission control rolling out, we expect 429s on best-effort; burn alerts should focus on enterprise SLI outcomes.

Links:
- Linear: ENG-4926 (Dashboards/alerts for protected routes), PM-882 (Enterprise SLO policy)
- Related PRs (for context): redwood/pr-27441 (429 reason codes), observability-pack/pr-1832 (dashboards)

## What...

title: oncall-memory-journal-and-telemetry-coalescer
preview: Motivation: On-call responders frequently need quick access to recent human decisions, triage notes, and distilled telemetry trends. This PR introduces a lightweight oncall-memory journal plus a telemetry coalescer to reduce noisy signal volume and surface concise, actionable summaries to the incident-bot and slo-to...
text:
Motivation: On-call responders frequently need quick access to recent human decisions, triage notes, and distilled telemetry trends. This PR introduces a lightweight oncall-memory journal plus a telemetry coalescer to reduce noisy signal volume and surface concise, actionable summaries to the incident-bot and slo-toolkit dashboards.

What this change does (single-string description): Adds an append-only oncall memory service (daemon + small embedded store) that journals short annotations from responders and automated synthesis hooks; provides a compact HTTP API (/v1/memory/append, /v1/memory/query) and a CLI (slo-memory export --range) for exporting session summaries. Implements a telemetry coalescer that consumes existing telemetry streams (metrics/traces/log-keys) and produces pre-aggregated "event shards" using configurable fingerprinting rules (by service, alert-key, tag-set) and ...

title: Add rollback gate rule library (error-rate + p95 latency) with defaults + overrides
preview: Context: As part of Safe apply + rollback for Optimization Configs, we need a consistent way to evaluate rollout gates across services/environments. Prior implementations hard-coded thresholds and metric queries per rollout controller, which made it difficult to tune defaults, apply consistent tagging requirements (...
text:
Context: As part of Safe apply + rollback for Optimization Configs, we need a consistent way to evaluate rollout gates across services/environments. Prior implementations hard-coded thresholds and metric queries per rollout controller, which made it difficult to tune defaults, apply consistent tagging requirements (tenant/model/region/config_version), and explain gate decisions in the Console/runbooks.

This PR adds a small, reusable “gate rule library” to slo-toolkit with two initial rules used by the canary rollout controller:
- Error rate (5xx) gate
- p95 latency gate

Key points:
- Sane defaults aimed at Hosted/Dedicated canaries, with explicit override hooks for Dedicated/private installs.
- A single evaluation result object that includes a user-facing explanation string, the metric query/selector used, the time window, and the exact threshold evaluated.
- Strict tag requirements...

title: Add per-model performance SLO templates (p95 latency + tokens/sec) for perf-canary + rollout gates
preview: This PR introduces first-class SLO Toolkit templates for per-model performance signals that we can reuse across regions/tier pools and plug into perf-canary alerting + rollout gates. We currently have ad-hoc Grafana + PromQL alerts in observability-pack that are hard to keep consistent, and they don't encode our rec...
text: This PR introduces first-class SLO Toolkit templates for per-model performance signals that we can reuse across regions/tier pools and plug into perf-canary alerting + rollout gates. We currently have ad-hoc Grafana + PromQL alerts in observability-pack that are hard to keep consistent, and they don't encode our recommended dampening/min-samples patterns. These templates are intended to standardize how we express and reason about performance regressions (latency and throughput) without turning performance into a classical "error budget" SLO. Scope: (1) Add new template families: ModelLatencyRegression and ModelThroughputRegression. (2) Provide guidance in-template for threshold selection (per model + SKU + region), minimum sample size, and dampening windows. (3) Add a small helper to keep label cardinality safe (standard label allowlist + enforced aggregation). (4) Add example configs...

title: Add weekly status accuracy audit job (incident tags vs Statuspage updates)
preview: Motivation: We’ve had recurring cases where the public status page didn’t reflect the impacted capability/region (e.g., streaming-only or embeddings-only degradations) even though the incident was tagged correctly internally, and vice-versa. This PR adds a lightweight weekly audit job to detect mismatches between (a...
text: Motivation: We’ve had recurring cases where the public status page didn’t reflect the impacted capability/region (e.g., streaming-only or embeddings-only degradations) even though the incident was tagged correctly internally, and vice-versa. This PR adds a lightweight weekly audit job to detect mismatches between (a) incident metadata (component_id/region/capability + severity + impact window) and (b) the Statuspage incident updates/components that were actually published. The goal is not to page or create more on-call work; it’s to produce a small, actionable report for follow-up during business hours and feed the status page transparency success metric (accuracy audits). What it does: 1) Runs weekly (Mon 09:00 PT) as a Kubernetes CronJob in the internal tooling cluster. 2) Pulls incidents from the incident timeline export endpoint (incident-bot export) for the prior 7 days, filterin...

title: Add component burn-rate alerts keyed by public status components (paging + runbook links)
preview: Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, we need component-level signals that are (a) consistent with our public component taxonomy and (b) actionable for on-call. This PR adds multi-window, multi-burn burn-rate alerts keyed by status component labels (component_id, region, capability) and routes p...
text: Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, we need component-level signals that are (a) consistent with our public component taxonomy and (b) actionable for on-call. This PR adds multi-window, multi-burn burn-rate alerts keyed by status component labels (component_id, region, capability) and routes pages to SRE on-call with direct links to the status page update runbook + per-component dashboards. Motivation: Recent incidents showed we were slow to declare partial impact (e.g., Streaming only, or us-east only) because our paging was still too service-oriented (gateway/scheduler) and didn’t map cleanly to the customer-facing component list. These alerts are intended to improve time-to-detection + reduce comms ambiguity by making the component/region impacted explicit from the first page. What’s included: 1) New alert rule groups for the public status components (Hosted ...
links: Linear: ENG-4841 (Add component-level SLO burn alerts)

title: Standardize SLO labels for public status components (component_id, region, capability)
preview: Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, we’re mapping internal signals (SLO burn, error%, latency p95/p99) to customer-visible status components. The status page component taxonomy is keyed on (component_id, region, capability). Today our SLO definitions in slo-toolkit are inconsistent: some use `...
text:
Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, we’re mapping internal signals (SLO burn, error%, latency p95/p99) to customer-visible status components. The status page component taxonomy is keyed on (component_id, region, capability). Today our SLO definitions in slo-toolkit are inconsistent: some use `component`, others use `public_component`, `route_family`, or `region_name`, and capability is sometimes inferred from the SLI name. This makes it hard to (a) drive component-scoped burn alerts, (b) build consistent dashboards, and (c) correlate incidents/status updates with SLO impact.

This PR standardizes labels across all public-facing SLOs to emit:
- `component_id`: stable identifier matching the status component registry (e.g., `hosted_api`, `streaming`, `embeddings`, `reranking`, `console`)
- `region`: stable region key (e.g., `us-east`, `us-west`, `eu-west`) as used...

title: Add overload error classification rules (separate from 5xx) for Hosted API SLO reporting
preview: Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding rollout, the gateway and downstream services now emit stable overload semantics (HTTP 429/503 + a Redwood error code + Retry-After). Our current SLO classifier treats most 5xx as availability failures, which makes overload-induced shedding look like an ...
text:
Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding rollout, the gateway and downstream services now emit stable overload semantics (HTTP 429/503 + a Redwood error code + Retry-After). Our current SLO classifier treats most 5xx as availability failures, which makes overload-induced shedding look like an outage and obscures the intent of graceful degradation. This PR updates slo-toolkit classification rules so overload errors are tracked separately from generic 5xx while still being visible in dashboards/alerts.

What this changes: Adds a new classifier bucket `overload` and rules to map requests into it based on (1) the canonical Redwood error code (from `x-redwood-error-code` header or JSON body `error.code`) and (2) the overload HTTP status mapping (429/503 with overload code). Removes these responses from the default 5xx classifier so they no longer inflate `server_erro...

title: Add time-to-ready SLI calculation (provisioning -> Ready) from event timestamps
preview:
Title: Add time-to-ready SLI calculation (provisioning -> Ready) from event timestamps

Context
We’re rolling out provision-to-ready automation for Dedicated pools, and we need a canonical SLI for “time-to-ready” that is consistent across regions/GPU types and robust to retries/quarantine. This PR adds an SLI calcul...
text:
Title: Add time-to-ready SLI calculation (provisioning -> Ready) from event timestamps

Context
We’re rolling out provision-to-ready automation for Dedicated pools, and we need a canonical SLI for “time-to-ready” that is consistent across regions/GPU types and robust to retries/quarantine. This PR adds an SLI calculator in slo-toolkit that derives duration from the event stream produced by the provisioning orchestrator and fleet readiness service.

What this PR does
- Introduces a new SLI: time_to_ready_seconds (aka provision_to_ready_seconds) computed per node instance based on event timestamps.
- Uses event timestamps rather than scrape-time to avoid skew and to survive delayed ingestion.
- Defines the start and end markers as:
 - start: first Observed(Allocated) or ProvisioningStarted event for a node (prefers Allocated when present)
 - end: first Ready event (terminal Ready transi...

title: Add incident timeline export (v1) for postmortems + incident-bot attachments
preview:
Context
We keep repeating the same manual work during incidents + postmortems: someone screenshots dashboards, someone copies a deploy timeline, someone pastes 12 links, and we still don’t have a consistent machine-readable timeline that incident-bot (or postmortem tooling) can attach.

This PR adds a v1 incident ti...
text:
Context
We keep repeating the same manual work during incidents + postmortems: someone screenshots dashboards, someone copies a deploy timeline, someone pastes 12 links, and we still don’t have a consistent machine-readable timeline that incident-bot (or postmortem tooling) can attach.

This PR adds a v1 incident timeline export format to slo-toolkit so that:
- incident-bot v2 can attach a lightweight timeline artifact (or deep link) in the first 5 minutes
- postmortem authors can import the same artifact into Confluence templates without reformatting
- we have a stable, versioned contract for future automation (e.g., “similar incident” matching)

What’s included
1) New export format: `incident_timeline_export_v1`
- Versioned: `schema_version: "1"`
- Ordered events with timestamps (RFC3339) and normalized types
- Designed to be tenant-safe by default: no raw request payloads, no secre...

title: Add error budget burn link generator (service/route/region)
preview: Summary: This PR adds a link generator for Redwood SLO error budget burn dashboards. The intent is to standardize deep links used by incident-bot v2 and incident runbooks so responders land on the right service/route/region with a consistent time window and the correct burn-rate thresholds. Background: During recent...
text: Summary: This PR adds a link generator for Redwood SLO error budget burn dashboards. The intent is to standardize deep links used by incident-bot v2 and incident runbooks so responders land on the right service/route/region with a consistent time window and the correct burn-rate thresholds. Background: During recent incidents we had a mix of hand-rolled Grafana URLs (some missing route filters, some using the wrong dashboard UID, and a few with query params that broke on URL encoding). ENG-5424 calls for a single generator that the bot can use to produce links per region and route, with guardrails to avoid unbounded cardinality and to keep the URL stable even as dashboard internals evolve. What’s included: (1) A small TypeScript library `src/links/errorBudgetBurn.ts` that generates error budget burn URLs given `service`, optional `route`, optional `region`, optional `tenantTier` (for ...

title: Add Runtime LTS SLO dashboards + rollout alerting
preview:
Context
Runtime LTS (runtime-1.18-lts) needs dedicated observability so we can (a) monitor error budget + latency specifically for LTS customer traffic, and (b) safely run patch rollouts with clear guardrails and fast detection of regressions. Existing dashboards aggregate across channels and mask LTS-specific signa...
text:
Context
Runtime LTS (runtime-1.18-lts) needs dedicated observability so we can (a) monitor error budget + latency specifically for LTS customer traffic, and (b) safely run patch rollouts with clear guardrails and fast detection of regressions. Existing dashboards aggregate across channels and mask LTS-specific signals, especially during patch week when canaries are running side-by-side with stable traffic.

This PR adds a small, opinionated LTS dashboard pack and alert rules to slo-toolkit. It is intended to be used by Release Eng + SRE during LTS patch releases and by on-call during steady-state to separate LTS from mainline noise.

What’s in this PR
1) Dashboards (Grafana)
- Runtime LTS Overview
 - Request rate (RPS), error rate, availability burn, and saturation signals scoped to LTS traffic
 - Latency heatmaps + p50/p95/p99 for LTS (by region, model family, and deployment mode whe...

title: Burn-rate alerts for Dedicated throughput SLO (recommended thresholds + templates)
preview: Context: As part of Dedicated throughput guarantees, we need consistent burn-rate alerting across regions/customers that matches the SLO semantics defined in the throughput SLO evaluator (PR #311). Today each service/team has been hand-rolling rule expressions and thresholds (with mismatched windows and inconsistent...
text:
Context: As part of Dedicated throughput guarantees, we need consistent burn-rate alerting across regions/customers that matches the SLO semantics defined in the throughput SLO evaluator (PR #311). Today each service/team has been hand-rolling rule expressions and thresholds (with mismatched windows and inconsistent annotations), which is creating alert noise and complicating incident triage.

This PR adds burn-rate alert generation helpers specifically for the Dedicated Throughput SLO signal ("throughput floor met" / "below floor"), plus recommended default thresholds and alert templates (labels/annotations/runbook links). The goal is to make it trivial for downstream repos (observability-pack, infra-config) to generate standard multi-window burn-rate alerts and keep them aligned with SLO semantics.

What’s included: (1) New helper: generate_burnrate_alerts_for_throughput_slo(...) th...

title: Add SLOs for Private backup job success + restore drill latency (with burn-rate alerts)
preview: Title: Add SLOs for Private backup job success + restore drill latency (with burn-rate alerts) Context: As part of the Private Day-2 backup/restore + DR readiness work, we need first-class SLOs that can be consumed by SRE + on-call automation (and mirrored into customer-facing dashboards where applicable). This PR a...
text: Title: Add SLOs for Private backup job success + restore drill latency (with burn-rate alerts) Context: As part of the Private Day-2 backup/restore + DR readiness work, we need first-class SLOs that can be consumed by SRE + on-call automation (and mirrored into customer-facing dashboards where applicable). This PR adds SLO definitions and standard burn-rate alerting hooks for: (1) backup job success rate and (2) automated restore drill latency. These SLOs are intended to be used by Redwood SRE for Redwood-managed Private environments and as reference defaults for customer deployments running our observability pack. Goals: - Provide consistent SLI/SLO definitions with clear scopes/labels - Wire standard multi-window burn-rate alerts (fast + slow) - Avoid noisy paging for non-prod while preserving signal for prod/private - Document assumptions + required metrics emitting behavior Depend...
